title: Dussehra (Vijayadashami)
intro: Dussehra, also known as Vijayadashami, is one of the most important and widely celebrated Hindu festivals.
description:
Dussehra, also known as Vijayadashami, is one of the most important and widely celebrated Hindu festivals. It is observed on the Dashami (tenth day) of the Shukla Paksha in the Hindu month of Ashwin, usually falling in September or October. The festival symbolizes the victory of good over evil, righteousness over injustice, and truth over falsehood.

According to Hindu traditions, Dussehra commemorates Lord Rama’s victory over the demon king Ravana after rescuing Goddess Sita, as described in the Ramayana. The day also marks Goddess Durga’s victory over the buffalo demon Mahishasura after a fierce nine-day battle. These sacred events represent the triumph of courage, devotion, and Dharma over evil forces.

Dussehra is celebrated with great enthusiasm across India through Ramlila performances, the burning of effigies of Ravana, Meghnad, and Kumbhakarna, temple worship, community celebrations, and cultural events. Many people also perform Ayudha Puja (worship of tools and weapons), worship vehicles and business instruments, and begin new ventures on this auspicious day. The festival inspires devotees to overcome ego, negativity, and injustice while embracing truth, righteousness, and virtue.

Festival Month

Ashwin (September – October)

Significance / Importance

Dussehra holds immense religious, spiritual, and cultural significance in Hinduism. The festival commemorates the victory of Lord Rama over Ravana and Goddess Durga’s triumph over Mahishasura, symbolizing the ultimate victory of righteousness over evil. It reminds devotees that truth, courage, devotion, and justice always prevail.

Vijayadashami is also considered one of the most auspicious days to begin new ventures, education, business activities, and spiritual practices. Many devotees perform Ayudha Puja, worship tools, vehicles, and professional instruments, and seek divine blessings for success, prosperity, protection, and happiness. The festival encourages everyone to eliminate negativity and follow the path of Dharma.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early, take a holy bath, and wear clean clothes.
2. Worship Lord Rama and Goddess Durga with devotion.
3. Offer flowers, incense, lamps, fruits, sweets, and other sacred offerings.
4. Chant Shri Ram Mantras, Durga Mantras, or recite the Ramayana and Durga scriptures.
5. Perform Ayudha Puja by worshipping tools, vehicles, weapons, or work equipment.
6. Perform Aarti and seek blessings for success and prosperity.
7. Participate in Ramlila celebrations or witness the burning of Ravana’s effigy.
8. Pray for victory over obstacles, negativity, and injustice.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Dussehra Katha
* Vijayadashami Katha
* Shri Ram Mantra
* Durga Mantra
* Shri Ram Aarti
* Durga Aarti
* Shri Ram Chalisa
* Durga Chalisa

faq.a1: Dussehra is celebrated on the Dashami (tenth day) of the Shukla Paksha in the Hindu month of Ashwin.
faq.a2: It celebrates Lord Rama’s victory over Ravana and Goddess Durga’s victory over Mahishasura, symbolizing the triumph of good over evil.
faq.q3: Why is Dussehra also called Vijayadashami?
faq.a3: The name Vijayadashami means “the tenth day of victory,” commemorating the victory of righteousness over evil.
faq.q4: In which month is Dussehra celebrated?
faq.a4: Dussehra usually falls in September or October.
faq.q5: Which deities are worshipped on Dussehra?
faq.a5: Lord Rama and Goddess Durga are primarily worshipped on Dussehra.
faq.q6: Why are Ravana’s effigies burned on Dussehra?
faq.a6: The burning of Ravana’s effigy symbolizes the destruction of evil, ego, and injustice, and the victory of truth and righteousness.
faq.q7: Is Dussehra an auspicious day to start new work?
faq.a7: Yes. Vijayadashami is considered one of the most auspicious days to begin new ventures, education, business, and other important activities.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Dussehra?
faq.a8: Dussehra symbolizes the victory of righteousness, truth, courage, and devotion over evil, inspiring people to lead a virtuous and meaningful life.
